definition: Ability to monitor evaluate remember and revise decisions based on feedback evidence and experience
measurement_note: Use monitoring quality after-action implementation policy revisions scenario updates training institutional memory and evaluation use

definition: Ability to adapt rules standards plans investments and operating modes under changing conditions
measurement_note: Use legal revision pathways adaptive standards decision triggers sunset clauses pilots emergency review and flexible finance

definition: Ability to align actors across sectors jurisdictions scales and knowledge systems
measurement_note: Use interagency agreements shared data joint exercises role clarity conflict resolution polycentric linkages and boundary organizations

definition: Ability to combine scientific local Indigenous practitioner and lived knowledge
measurement_note: Use co-production processes knowledge protocols community-defined indicators joint fact-finding and translation capacity

definition: Perceived fairness trust transparency public acceptability and consent around adaptive decisions
measurement_note: Use trust surveys participation outcomes complaint response public communication perceived fairness and decision transparency

definition: Ability to review constrain correct and explain adaptive authority
measurement_note: Use audits appeals public reporting oversight due process rights review anti-capture safeguards and implementation tracking

definition: Extent to which adaptive governance protects vulnerable groups and reduces unequal risk burdens
measurement_note: Use distributional analysis anti-displacement safeguards accessibility rights protection participation resources and burden tracking

definition: Administrative political legal technical financial and civic burden required to implement a governance strategy
measurement_note: Higher values should trigger feasibility review capacity planning legal review and sequencing analysis
